beats.narration: Every shot poses two questions — what radiation, and how much. The answers draw a line on the ground that the public never crosses.

payload.title: THE LINE YOU CONTROL
payload.sub: Survey it. Post it. Watch it.

beats.narration: Type comes from your source and its scatter. Quantity is dose rate, on a calibrated meter. The numbers you measure set every boundary.

payload.heading: Two questions, before the first shot

beats.narration: Inside the rope, zones carry legal lines. Past five millirem an hour at thirty centimeters: radiation area. Past one hundred: high radiation area. Each demands exact posting.

payload.heading: Nested zones, drawn by the meter
payload.note: 5 / 100 mrem IN 1 HOUR AT 30 cm — THE LEGAL LINES

beats.narration: The trefoil rules every sign — magenta on yellow, prescribed words. Calculation predicts where the lines fall; only walking the full perimeter with a meter proves it.
And one crew member watches the boundary every second. If anyone crosses, the source goes home first. Everything else comes second.

payload.sub: Someone crosses? Source in first — then deal with them.
